questions.question: The divergence of F = x²î + y²ĵ + z²k̂ at the point (1, 2, 3) is
If F = ∇φ for some scalar φ, then F is called
questions.explanation: If F = ∇φ (gradient of a scalar), F is conservative (also called irrotational since curl F = curl(∇φ) = 0). Solenoidal means divergence-free (div F = 0).

questions.question: By Green's theorem, ∮_C (y dx - x dy) over the unit circle equals
The curl of F = yî + xĵ + 0k̂ is
questions.explanation: curl F = (∂R/∂y - ∂Q/∂z)î + (∂P/∂z - ∂R/∂x)ĵ + (∂Q/∂x - ∂P/∂y)k̂. Here P=y, Q=x, R=0: = 0î + 0ĵ + (1-1)k̂ = 0. F is irrotational.

questions.question: Gauss's divergence theorem relates

options.A: Volume integral of divergence to surface flux integral
options.B: Line integral to surface integral of curl
options.C: Line integral to area integral in 2D
options.D: Surface integral to another surface integral

questions.explanation: Gauss's theorem: ∭_V (∇·F)dV = ∯_S F·dS. It converts a volume integral (of divergence) to a closed surface integral (flux). Stokes' converts line to surface; Green's converts line to area.
